content.problemStatement:
Chef is playing a game with his brother Chefu. He asked Chefu to choose a positive integer NNN, multiply it by a given integer AAA, then choose a divisor of NNN (possibly NNN itself) and add it to the product. Let's denote the resulting integer by MMM; more formally, M=A⋅N+dM = A \cdot N + dM=A⋅N+d, where ddd is some divisor of NNN.

Chefu told Chef the value of MMM and now, Chef should guess NNN. Help him find all values of NNN which Chefu could have chosen.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers AAA and MMM.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print two lines. The first line should contain a single integer CCC denoting the number of possible values of NNN. The second line should contain CCC space-separated integers denoting all possible values of NNN in increasing order.

It is guaranteed that the sum of CCC over all test cases does not exceed 10710^7107.
